author: @Soufriere84

text: @Soufriere84  Keep in mind that this show was made in the 1990s, long before Digital animation was a thing. So for each scene, including the classroom and the characters, and even animating Arnold chewing, took weeks to even get that scene done, let alone an episode made nearly 5-8 weeks before it aired, and once they plastered it on the cell sheet, either they would have to redraw the whole thing, backing up production by another week, or keep the mistake in. That is what's great about cell animation is you often see errors in tv shows that nowadays are cleaned up by simply deleting the layer or the character after being drawn. On actual celluloid sheets it would have to be redrawn to fix that mistake, let alone having to re-shoot the background again.

text: @nathanielcoleman5694  1990s computer augmented animation was early in its infancy... and wouldn't help you if your project was reusing assets across episodes.
